column1: TIER 1  |  WILD FOUNDATIONS  |  $50  |  7 Modules  |  ~90 Minutes Total
Target: Complete beginners. Walks through the 7-Night Protocol in structured video format. Ends with community bridge to Discord.

column6: Student understands WHY WILD is a cognitive performance tool, not a spiritual practice. Sets correct expectations.
column9: REM neuroscience overview. What happens in REM. Prefrontal reactivation. Norepinephrine drop. Historical cases: Tesla, Edison, McCartney. What WILD is and is not.
column12: Download and read the 7-Night Protocol PDF. Set up your tracking template. Write down one specific use case for your lucid training.

column6: Student implements circadian optimization protocol. Correctly executes morning light, evening wind-down, and sleep timing.
column9: Circadian clock mechanics. Suprachiasmatic nucleus. Morning light protocol (10,000 lux within 30 min of waking). Evening blue light elimination. Consistent sleep onset. Pre-sleep 60-min downregulation sequence. WBTB timing explained.
column12: Complete the 60-min pre-sleep protocol tonight. Log attempt time and sleep quality in tracker. Note anything unusual about the hypnagogic phase.

column6: Student can identify hypnagogic phenomena and hold awareness in the threshold state without falling unconscious or snapping awake.
column9: What hypnagogia is. Visual, auditory, sensory hallucinations. Hypnic jerks. The three anchor techniques: Breath, Sensory, Visual. How to observe without reacting. The WILD entry window (10-30 min). WBTB technique introduction.
column12: Practice breath anchor technique for 15 minutes tonight. Log which anchor feels most natural. Note what hypnagogic phenomena you observed.

column6: Student reframes sleep paralysis as success, not threat. Can execute stabilization protocol and transition from SP into lucid dream.
column9: Sleep paralysis physiology (glycine/GABA inhibition). Why threatening imagery occurs. Ventromedial medulla explained. 5-step SP stabilization protocol. Breathing to activate parasympathetic. 30-90 second wait window. Panic reframe: arousal as fuel.
column12: Read the SP section of the Protocol PDF again. Visualize the stabilization protocol before bed. Log whether SP occurred and what you did.

column6: Student can maintain lucid awareness for 5-30 minutes without dream collapse. Knows 3 grounding techniques and how to use lucid time productively.
column9: Why lucidity collapses (arousal spike). Sensory grounding: touch ground, rub hands, spin. Vocal declaration ("Increase clarity"). Setting specific pre-sleep intentions. Using lucid time for mental rehearsal, problem solving, skill practice. Directing dream environment.
column12: Before bed: write one specific intention (problem, skill, or scenario). After waking: voice-record dream details immediately. Log lucidity duration and what you did.

column6: Student executes a full WILD session with deliberate cognitive intent. Integrates insights via journaling and understands how to interpret results.
column9: Pre-sleep precision intention setting. Visualization before sleep. The performance window: what to do during lucidity. Integration journaling (4 questions on waking). Results interpretation: 14/43/71% benchmarks. Common failure modes and fixes.
column12: Complete the full 7-night tracking template. Calculate your success rate. Write a one-paragraph summary of what you achieved and what you will repeat.

column2: Your Next 30 Days: Building the Skill
column6: Student has a clear 30-day roadmap for continued practice. Understands how to cycle the protocol and how community supports progress.
column9: 15-20% improvement per cycle. How to run subsequent 7-night cycles. When to troubleshoot vs. continue. Introduction to Discord community: how to use it for accountability. Preview of Tier 2 advanced techniques. Community challenge structure.
column12: Join the Discord server (link in course). Post your Week 1 results in #wild-protocol-results. Set your next 7-night cycle start date.

column4: Webcam face cam + screen share for visuals. Clean background or minimal desk setup. Loom (free) works for all 7 modules.
Brain diagram (REM/prefrontal). Sleep cycle graph (NREM/REM). Hypnagogia visual (transition state graphic). 7-night protocol overview slide.
Clinical, precise, confident. Like a performance coach, not a wellness guru. No spirituality. No woo. Reference wearable data and neuroscience.
All script content is in WILD_Phase_One_Marketing_Funnel.docx under Lead Magnet Full Text. Expand each night into module format.
Gumroad for fast launch (3-4 days to set up). Teachable/Kajabi if building full LMS. Skool if combining course + community in one platform.
Use the hook formulas from Marketing Funnel doc. Lead with "730 hours of wasted REM per year." Add social proof once first students complete.
